title: How Space Ripples Might Have Made Dark Matter
summary: Scientists have a new idea about how dark matter was made. Dark matter is a mysterious substance that makes up most of the universe, but we can't see it. The new idea says that ripples in space, called gravitational waves, might have helped create dark matter. These ripples happened right after the Big Bang, when the universe was very young. The ripples turned into tiny particles that later became dark matter. This is important because we don't know what dark matter is made of. If this idea is correct, it could help us understand the universe better. The scientists who came up with this idea are Professor Joachim Kopp and Dr. Azadeh Maleknejad. They published their study in a science journal called Physical Review Letters. They plan to do more calculations to see if their idea is right. This research might solve one of the biggest mysteries in science.

Ripples in space that happen when big events like black holes crash together
The event that started the universe about 13.8 billion years ago
Tiny pieces that everything in the universe is made of

background_read: The universe is everything around us, including stars, planets, and space. Most of the universe is made of something we cannot see called dark matter. Scientists are trying to figure out what dark matter is. This article talks about a new idea that uses gravitational waves, which are ripples in space, to explain how dark matter might have been made.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists Joachim Kopp and Azadeh Maleknejad
WHAT: They think gravitational waves may have created dark matter
WHERE: The early universe, right after the Big Bang
WHY: To help solve the mystery of what dark matter is

perspectives.description: They believe this new idea could explain dark matter.
Some might think more evidence is needed.
